programmes.show_description: This week's dating line up includes Mim and Jack, Esther and Liam, Caoimhe and Will and Marcin and Cavan.
Ryan is determined to celebrate his 12th birthday with glamour and style. But it looks like Mam's erratic behaviour might derail the night.
No Going Back: Alma is back in town and desperate for things to be different. She bags herself a talent agent, who turns out to be a bit of a blagger.
Dawn makes a tough decision. A guilty TK spreads a secret. Harper and Drew feel the pressure.
Remi's rocking out in front of the Surf Club but notices he's getting pins and needles in his hands.
Sarah McInerney, Dáithí O'Sé and Emer O'Neill are joined by reporter Thomas Crosse to present live coverage of the national St. Patrick's Day Parade on the streets of Dublin.
The Plague: Bishop Brennan descends on Craggy Island to investigate rumours that Father Jack has started sleepwalking in the nude - only to come face to face with his worst fear.
The Rusty Mackerel, Teelin: Anna Clifford and travel influencer Éadaoin Fitzmaurice journey to Donegal to visit The Rusty Mackerel, a pub nestled in the foothills of Sliabh Liag.
Marie Antoinette's dangerous sexual affair with Fersen is reignited.
Eliza and Pete remain at odds as they work on an embarrassing case together. Wayne struggles to turn his professional switch off while looking after Kalthi.
DCI Jo Davidson faces increasing pressure from all sides as AC-12 make major breakthroughs in the investigation.

programmes.show_name: Hell For Leather - The Story of Gaelic Football
programmes.show_description: The role of Gaelic football in Ireland's sporting, cultural and social history. This first edition looks back in time at the rough and tumble origins of the sport. (SL)
Dawn decides to make her move. Harper plays happy families. Maeve's up to her old tricks.
Both Abigail and Mali are excited that her return to work means she can take out her frustrations on customers, instead of her boyfriend.
A new teacher at school named Mr. Lassen is determined to crush Bart Simpson's spirit.
Darragh Maloney is joined by Didi Hamann and Richie Sadlier for live coverage of Liverpool v Galatasaray in Round of 16, 2nd Leg. Commentary: Des Curran & Ray Houghton KO 8.00pm
